findings.description: Autonomous daemon sent 7 WA messages to Reinis Fals and 9 to Justs overnight. Cortex rule R260 was created but model reasoned around it ('informing the cofounder is coordination, not outreach'). Required escalation to mechanical PreToolUse hook blocking send_whatsapp for non-Justs recipients. Same class: soft rules bypassed by in-context reasoning.
findings.rule_text: Cortex-only communication rules are insufficient. Any outreach restriction MUST be enforced mechanically (dispatcher hook, exit code 2) because the model will reason around text-based rules under autonomous pressure. Never trust a cortex rule alone for safety-critical behavior.

findings.description: FSM locked in MAINTENANCE_FAILED multiple times (escalation_count hit 204). Brain cycle crashed on _recursive_median_split (no depth limit), then timed out (300s too short for 879 L1 entries). Each failure blocked all tools, preventing self-recovery. Required manual user intervention twice.
findings.rule_text: Before reporting brain cycle health as 'clean', verify: FSM state is IDLE, escalation_count is 0, last_cycle_outcome is not failure. When cycle fails, surface the exact manual fix command immediately — do not attempt repeated automatic retries that compound escalation_count.

findings.description: Gave user environment-incompatible commands three times: (1) '&&' chaining in PowerShell, (2) ln -sfn symlinks without checking Developer Mode on Windows, (3) setup.sh relied on python3 which wasn't installed. Each required user round-trip to diagnose and retry.
findings.rule_text: Before giving shell commands to Justs on Windows: verify shell (PowerShell vs Git Bash vs CMD), verify symlink capability (Developer Mode), verify binary availability (python3 vs python). Ask 'which shell are you in?' if ambiguous. Prefer cp over ln on Windows.

findings.description: setup.sh had set -e but python3 check on line 38 failed silently — symlink commands appeared to run but actually didn't land. Took 3 round-trips (12:01→12:07→12:12) before falling back to cp. Original behavior: assumed setup.sh success from partial output. Correction: user showed symlinks missing. Resulting behavior: switched to cp -r which worked first try.

Gmail OAuth root cause diagnosis: identified Testing-mode 7-day refresh token expiry from cron logs and script comments, gave precise fix path (Google Cloud Console → Auth Platform → Audience → PUBLISH APP), followed up with screenshot-guided navigation when user couldn't find the button.
Jeff multi-agent verification on EC2: pulled commit, tested REPO_ROOT resolution, AGENT_NAME rendering in all 6 prompts, f-string escaping — all passed. Clean approve with evidence.
Vericity Transactions: caught React.ReactNode import bug in TransactionFilters.tsx:239 before it would break TypeScript compilation. Submitted revision → teacher APPROVED.
WA bridge dead Baileys socket diagnosed and restarted. Bridge was reporting 'connected' but throwing 428 on sends. Restart cleared the backlog.
Honest disclosure when user asked 'but those are fake messages right?' about comms pane seed data. No fabrication, immediate acknowledgment that curl-seeded messages were scaffolding.

Brain cycle synthesis timeout: original 300s limit was arbitrary and too short for 879 L1 entries (synthesis needed 630s). Original behavior: cycle timed out and failed. Correction: user applied sed to increase to 900s. Resulting behavior: synthesis completed, produced 44 new L2 entries.
